riders.notes: Maiden voyage FB1750. vs 1400V2: much more efficient for LDP. Paddle-up 15% harder but worth it. 16C water, 2400m altitude.
77kg, beginner dock starts, intermediate wing+tow foiler. Community recommends dedicated wings per discipline - Spitfire 960 for wake, not PNG 1200.
Long distance pumping from dock or flatwater paddle-up. Mark Shinn recommends Skinny 25 at 72kg on short fuse.
~100kg rider using Fatty mast+fuse with foil drive. Mark Shinn (also ~100kg) confirms works well with all FD units.
Amos Nitro board has nose-up geometry. 1deg mast shim resolves: smoother takeoffs, better foot position. Key tip for Nitro owners.
91kg in full winter suit at 8C water, 11-17kn thermals. HPS 930 with 380 speed stab still works amazingly on 92L board with 5.0 wing. Comparing to ART V2 879 (new purchase).
Love love 830 with new ultrashort fuse, and surf stab 320 on 75L Blast, killer combo. One of the fastest, carviest craft on the water.
First and only Axis setup. Far more pitch sensitive than anything else I have ridden.
1350 is main quiver foil - can flatwater pump, paddle up, parawing or DW SUP DW. Rarely does trips without it.
Epic parawing downwind session. Impressed by speed range and maneuverability. Fireball 1350 allows acceleration when wind picks up on slow swell.
First session on FB1750. Carbon mast was expensive investment. 98kg rider.

equipment_mentioned.notes: Asking 820 vs 750 for versatility. Community strongly recommends 820.
82cm sweet spot for wing, surf, dock, tow. Extra length = breach prevention in rough seas/waves.
Has most Surge sizes. Recommends 830 for 85kg Gold Coast wave riding. SurfFx has demos.

riders.notes: HPS 930 + 380 speed stab at 91kg: super easy early flight, low stall speed, forgiving. Lacks glide vs ART V2 879. Good for messy water and recovery. 'Feels like cheating' for early flight.
riders.sentiment: positive on HPS 930 for beginner/easy conditions

riders.note: First session Fireball 1750 review. 2-3x longer runs vs smaller foils. Easier dockstart than expected. Long-distance foil. Prefers 358/25 over 358/35 stabilizer. Carbon mast over aluminum.

setup.notes: Previously rode 780s. Surge 830 approx 20-30% better performer than Spitfire or Art v2. Upgraded from 780 setup.

riders.note: Top contributor recommendation for 92kg winger: 20-30% better than ART v2

setup.notes: At 115kg: 780 for shoulder-high tow (need lots of wind to wing), 830 for hip-high, 890 for free ride winging

riders.note: Detailed weight-specific Surge sizing breakdown from top contributor

setup.notes: Described as go-to dockstart foil. Morning holiday routine at El Gouna

riders.performance: 830: 33.7km/hr top speed, steady, maneuverable. Prefers 830 over 890 in these conditions.
riders.otherThoughts: Also rides Surge 890 with same 300p; 890 feels twitchy above 30km/hr in waves
Prefers AV2 879 + SS320 over Surge in waves overall

setup.stab: Skinny 45 (preferred), Surf Skinny 300 (found tricky in chop)

riders.otherThoughts: SS300 tricky for intermediate in choppy; Skinny 45 awesome on 830; SS300 great on Surge 950

riders.performance: Impressed by speed range and maneuverability. Questions if Surge 1010 would excel in similar conditions.
riders.otherThoughts: Asking community for Surge 1010 split times in bay run conditions

wing.notes: Desperately waiting for the Surge 1150. Waiting for Surf Skinny 340 to pair with Surge 830.

riders.otherThoughts: Please get the Surge 1150 to the market since I have sold my SF 1180 and miss the light wind days with small bumps.

riders.location: Anacortes WA / Seattle WA / Hood River / La Ventana

wing.fuselage: US for waves, Short for speed or flat water
wing.notes: Easy to ride, pretty fast, good glide, reasonable turning

riders.otherThoughts: Been on Axis for 4 years. AV2 879 has been my favorite foil. Tried AV2 819 in La Ventana and want one for more speed. Would like standard Axis foils built for Tempo fuse.

wing.notes: Wide wind range, great glide, turns well, recover from breaches

riders.otherThoughts: Versatile options for mix n match

wing.notes: Tried and true, smooth, stable, turns well, holds a line at speed. Curious about ART V2 1099 and Fireball 1250.

riders.otherThoughts: Really want to try the ART V2 1099. Tried a Fireball 1350 once and miss the glide. Hope Live2Kite comes back soon.

wing.notes: Turns nice, neutral lift

prone.notes: I didn't know any better but it turns nicer than an HPS 980

wing.notes: Surfy. Looking forward to try Surge
Great low end and high end. Great carving and very good glide. Easy tip breaches.

dockStart.notes: Great for dock start as I am just learning. Love that an old front wing is still so relevant!

kite.notes: Only kitefoil light wind. Super glidy setup

parawing.notes: Awesome for upwind/downwind swell riding and downwinders

riders.otherThoughts: Please find local dealers in South Africa. It's a big market but Axis is missing. It's hard to get.

wing.notes: Very playful and predictable

prone.notes: Can link waves easy and it turns really good. Would recommend any and all axis prone riders to go on the surge

dockStart.notes: Easy to pump and take off and great for practicing turns

parawing.notes: Great low end and easy to turn - suitable for learning parawing

downwindSUP.notes: When conditions are good bc it's fast and still turns good

riders.otherThoughts: Would love to try Tempo. My recommendation would be to try other gear to compare and contrast.

wing.notes: Spitfire for waves and turbulence, 939 for speed/glide in calm

riders.otherThoughts: Considering Foil Drive or SUP foil as I am proficient at SUP surfing

wing.notes: Its the one I have and like
Great for waves and flat water. Surge is super playful, low stall speed, easy lift. Fireball 1070 is upwind/downwind tool.

prone.notes: Turns off the back foot like surfing. The Surge is a game changer.

wake.notes: The 1010/950 Surge are the ultimate wake foils. Easy lift, fast, surfy, handle prop wash and chop.

foilAssist.notes: Foil Drive with the Surge 1010/950 is epic in waves and wake. The Axis Pocket Trench is awesome!

riders.otherThoughts: There is always going to be something new from Axis that will be better. Try different foils. Foil Assist/Foil Drive is an amazing tool to test different foils.

wing.notes: Surfy and good control
Super loose and carvey but not switchy. Feel in control even at top speed. Ex 780SP rider.

riders.experience: 1-2 years (actually listed as such but answers suggest more)

downwindSUP.notes: Surge for steep wind-against-tide, Fireball for glide

supNonDW.notes: Fun for turns, able to link waves

parawing.notes: Particularly good in fun steep bumps like wind against tide

riders.otherThoughts: Still building quiver. Next purchase will be Fireball 1160 for faster ground swell downwind runs.

prone.notes: Great pump and glide. Turns well enough. Wide speed range.

wake.notes: Great pump and glide. Turns well enough.

towIn.notes: Closest feeling to riding a short board.

dockStart.notes: Fairly easy to dock start, turns well

longDistancePump.notes: Fairly easy to dock start, turns well

wing.notes: Good for surging reef break

foilAssist.notes: Easy to get on foil, can hold in reasonable size

riders.otherThoughts: Sticking with the Spitfire range. Get used to what you ride.

foil_drive.notes: Lake boat wave chasing. Mark Shinn says HM fine at 70kg with <100cm wings.

riders.source: Jason Robert Beck, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Feb 22 2026

wing.notes: Foil Drive in waves. Tim VR recommends Fireballs for upwind/DW FoilDrive.

foil_drive.notes: Debating sizing. Chris Pelly runs 890 FD + 1010 parawing combo.

riders.source: Matthew Kyhnn, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Mar 2 2026

wing.notes: Perfect board for winging. Stable, quick takeoff, nimble. Also uses 75L and 85L Blast in light winds.

riders.source: Vangelis Evan Mavridoglou, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Mar 8 2026

wing.notes: Loves it. Blast 20-30% easier to pop up than 75L Froth.

riders.source: Brad Gordon, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Mar 5 2026

wing.notes: DW wingfoil runs in 1-2.5ft bay chop. Andy Corbe recommends old Spitfire 1100/1180 or FB1250 for connecting bumps.

riders.source: Shaun Henderson, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Mar 3 2026

downwind_sup.notes: When conditions are good bc it's fast and still turns good just has a nice feel to it
Fast, plenty of pump and glide and still turns good
Surge for wind against tide steep conditions, Fireball for light winds. Love the surge for steeper surfy conditions and the fireball for its glide

prone_surf.notes: Would recommend any and all axis prone riders to go on the surge
Turns off the back foot like surfing. The Surge is a game changer. Great pump and glide. Wide speed range.

wing.notes: The 950/830 Surge are super playful, low stall speed, easy lift. 1070 is flat water or upwind/downwind tool. +20 fuse for bigger waves.
Desperately waiting for the Surge 1150. Waiting for Surf Skinny 340 to pair with upcoming Surge 830.
Fast, smooth, stable. Glides forever.

sup.notes: Epic setup on the new Dart 120L when it's tiny. Get into smallest waves and pump around forever. Perfect foil for tiny bumps. Endless pump and glide. Great turning.

foil_drive.notes: Foil Drive with the Surge 1010/950 is epic in waves and wake. The Axis Pocket Trench is awesome! Best foil drive setup.

riders.name: Survey Rider (Varberg, Sweden. Beats most spots😃)

setups.front_wing: Surge 890, 1010 desperately waiting for the Surge 1150
setups.rear_wing: 320 Surf skinny, Progressive 350,375 waiting for the Surf skinny 340 to pair with an upcoming Surge 830
setups.fuselage: Short advance+ and short advance +20. +20 is used in bigger waves.

setups.fuselage: US for waves, Short for speed or flat water

setups.mast: I rare the ultra 800 but ideally the 1050 for winging

setups.front_wing: Konrad lx760 (but will try a surge)

riders.location: Wakefoiling on Lewis smith lake in Alabama

setups.rear_wing: Skinny 35 but changing to skinny surf 43 (which work SO good with fireball 1250!!)

setups.front_wing: Depends on conditions.  Wind against tide then surge 1010.  Light winds fireball 1350
setups.rear_wing: Surf skinny 300 for wind against tide, or skinny 40

dockStart.notes: Asks for turning-focused progression from PNG 1300 V2 and ART Pro 1201 into surf starts.

wake.fuselage: Standard black fuse vs Black Advanced/Advance+ (community says standard works; advanced improves response)
wake.notes: Youth wake foil setup compatibility discussion.

riders.source: Mitch Batterham, AXIS Foil Riders FB, Mar 11 2026

riders.performance: 1010 + 450/67 dead easy for 215lb heavyweight winging. 350/63 ok but pitch surprises in chaotic conditions.
riders.otherThoughts: Very heavy rider needing large stab for Surge 1010

riders.conditions: Behind AXIS boat and MC x24
riders.performance: Rides SO 960 currently, asking about Surge 950
riders.otherThoughts: Advanced rider at 92kg behind direct drive boat wakes

riders.performance: Sillyshort fuse far less prone to breaching than Ultrashort for given wings. For 66kg, longer fuses very difficult to balance.
riders.otherThoughts: Each fuse increment roughly = one size rear wing in pitch control

riders.notes: Max speed 33.3km/hr on 890. Prefers 890 for smaller/longer section waves over 830. 25km/hr sweet zone.
830 too big over shoulder high at 66kg. Fluttery feeling at top speed. Considering 780 or 740.
890 sweet zone ~25km/hr. Gets challenged 30-35km/hr on solid swell. Testing 830 on bigger waves.
Praised Fireball 1350 + Skinny 45 for speed range and maneuverability in para-wing DW.
Mini Dart 80L perfect for 88kg winger. Stable, quick takeoff, nimble. AXIS/Vaikobi 2026 collab vest mentioned.

riders.name: Darren Kite
riders.notes: Max 33.3km/hr on 560m wave. Avg 25km/hr. Considering 830 for smaller days.

riders.name: Jonathan Robert
riders.notes: 830 surge feels too big for anything over shoulder high at 66kg. Planning to try Sillyshort A+.

riders.name: Tee Kay
riders.notes: Looking to upgrade to Surge 830 - loves lift/glide/speed but hates turning of ART V2

riders.name: Brad Gordon
riders.notes: At 96kg, transitioned from 780s. Estimates ~20-30% better than Spitfire or ART v2.

riders.name: Cedric Okp
riders.notes: Daily dock start routine, calls FB1350 his go-to dockstart foil.

setup.note: Seeking first surf wing - considering Surge or Spitfire

riders.notes: efoil rider transitioning to surf foiling. 18 sessions. Seeking sizing advice for Surge/Spitfire.

riders.notes: HPS 930 easy/low stall speed vs ART V2 879 better glide. Comparing old vs new setup in light wind. Very detailed session report.

riders.notes: 91kg in full wintersuit, HPS 930 still performs excellently vs newer ART V2

riders.notes: 820mm sweet spot for wing, surf, dock, tow in rough seas; extra length for breach prevention

frontWings.note: Broadest range, still most popular overall

frontWings.note: Rising fast, praised for surf feel

trends: Surge line appearing across all disciplines - praised for surf feel and turning
Ultrashort and Crazyshort fuselages dominate all disciplines
HM Carbon masts preferred; 90cm most popular for wing
Skinny/Surf Skinny rear wings are the standard
Parawing adoption at 30% - remarkable for emerging discipline
Strong demand for Surge 1150 (not yet released)
Fireball 1070 is the most versatile wing (appears in 5 disciplines)

setups.notes: First and only Axis setup. Very pitch sensitive vs other brands
FB1750 pitch sensitive even with ultrashort fuse
Weight-specific stab guidance for FB1750 dockstart vs flatwater
